item.title: Version 1.05
item.description:
What's New in 1.05

  • New: Conflict resolution sheet — when queueing files with naming conflicts, a new sheet shows all conflicts with editable filenames, real-time validation, and per-item remove buttons
  • New: Smart numeric suffixes — output collisions are resolved with _1, _2, etc. Re-queueing video_1.mov correctly increments to _2 instead of _1_1
  • New: Queue now shows the output filename instead of the source filename
  • New: You can now overwrite existing output files — the app warns and offers Replace, Rename, or Go Back instead of forcing a rename
  • Improved: Sources selection highlight for easier identification
  • Improved: Update checks on every launch
  • Improved: Audio codec validation — bit depth clamped to valid ranges for the selected codec
  • Improved: Output suffix text field is always editable
  • Fixed: Overlay and LUT settings lost when "Keep sources in staging" was enabled
  • Fixed: Burn-in timecode drift — frame counter now uses rounding instead of truncation
  • Fixed: Burn-in timecode mismatch when output frame rate differs from source
  • Fixed: Overlay scaling when output resolution differs from source
  • Fixed: AAC audio encoding failure with 32-bit bit depth
  • Fixed: False "overwrite source" warning when transcoding to a different container format

item.title: Version 1.04
item.description:
What's New in 1.04

  • New: Audio preview playback — hear audio while previewing video files (AVFoundation, MXF, MKV/WebM, BRAW, R3D)
  • New: Audio-only file preview — mp3, wav, flac, and other audio files now show a waveform view with playback controls
  • New: Volume slider and mute toggle in preview controls (persists across sessions)
  • New: Pause/Resume queue — truly suspends active encodes mid-frame and resumes from the same position
  • New: Toolbar shows icons with text labels, Add to Queue has Enter keyboard shortcut
  • New: Encode menu with Add to Queue shortcut
  • Fixed: Frame rate upconversion now works correctly for MP4/MOV files (was only fixed for MXF in v1.02)
  • Fixed: Window close during encoding no longer causes looping quit dialog or crash — app stays in dock, click to restore
  • Fixed: Partial output files cleaned up on cancel/quit (including AVAssetWriter journal files)
  • Fixed: Queue clear no longer leaves orphaned job in settings panel
  • Fixed: Cancel encoding shows confirmation dialog before cancelling
  • Fixed: Insta360 .insv/.lrv transcoding crash with missing AVChannelLayoutKey
  • Fixed: HEVC Main10 profile auto-downgrades to Main for 8-bit sources (prevents purple/white color corruption)
  • Fixed: BRAW framework embedding no longer fails on incremental builds
  • Changed: "Cost" terminology replaced with "Slots" in queue UI

item.title: Version 1.03
item.description:
What's New in 1.03

  • Fixed: Blackmagic RAW runtime is now embedded in the app — BRAW files no longer require a separate Blackmagic RAW SDK or Player install
  • Fixed: RED R3D decoding now falls back to OpenCL or CPU when Metal GPU is unavailable (e.g. virtual machines)
  • Fixed: Improved error messages when RAW codec initialization fails
  • Fixed: Settings window and console now render correctly on Light Mode systems
  • New: Welcome screen on first launch lets you enter a license key right away

item.title: Version 1.02
item.description:
What's New in 1.02

  • Fixed: MXF frame rate conversion no longer stalls on longer files (e.g. 24fps → 30fps)
  • Fixed: Frame rate upconversion now correctly duplicates frames to maintain duration
  • Fixed: DNx media color accuracy — output now matches source contrast and includes correct BT.709 color tags
  • New: Quit confirmation when encodes are running — no more accidental data loss
  • New: Dock icon progress bar shows encode progress while the app is in the background
  • New: Low frame rate options (5, 10, 15 fps) added to encoding settings
